tools.name: resolve_name
tools.description: Forward-resolve a .hood name to the address it points to. Returns null if unset.

tools.name: reverse_resolve
tools.description: Reverse-resolve an address to its primary .hood name. `verified` is true only when the name's forward record points back to the address.

tools.name: resolve_text
tools.description: Read a text record (e.g. "avatar", "url", "com.twitter") set on a .hood name.

tools.name: check_availability
tools.description: Check whether a .hood name is available to register, and its yearly price.

tools.name: name_info
tools.description: Full snapshot of a .hood name: resolved address, NFT owner, expiry, and availability.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: resolve_text · Private data: reverse_resolve, resolve_text · Public sink: resolve_name, reverse_resolve.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: resolve_text · Private data: reverse_resolve, resolve_text · Public sink: resolve_name, reverse_resolve

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
